D4 Colorimetry L5 Evaluate Reference

CMC(l:c) — The Tolerance Ellipse

drag a sample around a reference and see whether it falls inside the tilted, chroma-stretched acceptance ellipse the textile trade uses

CMC(l:c) shapes a tolerance ellipsoid around the reference: stretched along chroma, narrow across hue, and tilted to follow the radius. A sample passes when ΔE_CMC ≤ 1 — inside the ellipse on the a*b* plane and within the lightness band.

Move the sample's lightness and a*, b*. The ellipse is the ΔE_CMC = 1 acceptance boundary; the bar at right is the lightness band. Switch l:c between 2:1 (acceptability — the textile default) and 1:1 (perceptibility) and watch the lightness band resize.
